senses.definition: used to suggest that changing deeply ingrained habits can be difficult due to the comfort and familiarity associated with them

examples.x: Even though he had retired, John still woke up early every morning because old habits die hard.

senses.definition: used to suggest that negative or harmful habits can be particularly difficult to break or change, even when one knows they are harmful to one's well-being or success

examples.x: After years of overeating, it was difficult for Tom to lose weight because bad habits die hard.

senses.definition: used to imply that habits or actions that are repeated frequently become automatic and require less conscious effort, emphasizing that people should be mindful of their habits

examples.x: Mary had been practicing good posture for so long that she no longer had to think about sitting up straight, as they say habit is second nature.

word_list.word: the dog (always|) returns to its vomit
word_list.pronunciation: 칧톛 d틛톔틣토 틛톖틣lwe톩z 톷톩t틛톞틣nz t툵 톩ts v틛톔틣m톩t

senses.definition: used to imply that some people repeat harmful behavior despite knowing it is harmful to their well-being or success, emphasizing the importance of learning from past mistakes

word_list.word: go to bed with the lamb and rise with the lark
word_list.pronunciation: 토틟o툵 t톛 b틛톝d w톩칧칧톛 l틛칝m 칝nd 톷틛a톩z w톩칧칧톛 l틛톔틣톷k

senses.definition: used to imply that a consistent sleep schedule, where one goes to bed early and wakes up early, can lead to increased productivity and overall well-being

examples.x: Maria was training for a marathon, so she made it a habit to go to bed with the lamb and rise with the lark to ensure she had enough time to train and recover.

word_list.word: let the cobbler stick to his last
word_list.pronunciation: l틛톝t 칧톛 k틛톔틣bl톜 st틛톩k t톛 h톩z l틛칝st

senses.definition: used to suggest that individuals should focus on their area of expertise or specialization, rather than attempting to do things they are not skilled at, to avoid the risk of failure

examples.x: When it comes to designing the website, I'll leave it to the experts and let the cobbler stick to his last.

word_list.word: once a thief, always a thief
word_list.pronunciation: w틛툷ns 톓 풪틛i틣f 틛톖틣lwe톩z 톓 풪틛i틣f

senses.definition: used to suggest that someone who has a history of stealing or engaging in dishonest behavior is likely to continue doing so in the future, regardless of any attempts to change or reform
